sections.label: Section 1178
sections.original: τόδʼ ἔστʼ ἐκεῖνο , καὶ μάλʼ ἀθλίως ἔχον .
sections.translation: It is , though in a very wretched state .

sections.label: Section 1179
sections.original: οἴμοι ταλαίνης ἆρα τῆσδε συμφορᾶς .
sections.translation: What pity , then , for this miserable fortune !

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: οὐ δή ποτʼ , ὦ ξένʼ , ἀμφʼ ἐμοὶ στένεις τάδε ;
sections.translation: Surely , stranger , you are not saddened like this on my account ?

sections.label: Section 1181
sections.original: ὦ σῶμʼ ἀτίμως κἀθέως ἐφθαρμένον .
sections.translation: O frame dishonorably , godlessly wasted !

sections.label: Section 1182
sections.original: οὔτοι ποτʼ ἄλλην ἢ ʼμὲ δυσφημεῖς , ξένε .
sections.translation: Those ills of which you speak , stranger , are none other ’ s than mine .

sections.label: Section 1183
sections.original: φεῦ τῆς ἀνύμφου δυσμόρου τε σῆς τροφῆς ,
sections.translation: Ah , pity for your unwed , ill-fated life !

sections.label: Section 1184
sections.original: τί δή ποτʼ , ὦ ξένʼ , ὧδʼ ἐπισκοπῶν στένεις ;
sections.translation: Why , stranger , do you stare and grieve in this way ?

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: ὡς οὐκ ἄρʼ ᾔδη τῶν ἐμῶν οὐδὲν κακῶν .
sections.translation: How I knew nothing , it seems , of my own sorrows !

sections.label: Section 1186
sections.original: ἐν τῷ διέγνως τοῦτο τῶν εἰρημένων ;
sections.translation: What that has been said made you realize this ?

sections.label: Section 1187
sections.original: ὁρῶν σε πολλοῖς ἐμπρέπουσαν ἄλγεσιν .
sections.translation: It was the sight of you conspicuous in your many sufferings .

sections.label: Section 1188
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὁρᾷς γε παῦρα τῶν ἐμῶν κακῶν .
sections.translation: And yet you see but a few of my troubles .

sections.label: Section 1189
sections.original: καὶ πῶς γένοιτʼ ἂν τῶνδʼ ἔτʼ ἐχθίω βλέπειν ;
sections.translation: And how could there be any more odious to look on than these ?

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: ὁθούνεκʼ εἰμὶ τοῖς φονεῦσι σύντροφος
sections.translation: Because I share house and table with the murderers .

sections.label: Section 1191
sections.original: τοῖς τοῦ ; πόθεν τοῦτʼ ἐξεσήμηνας κακόν ;
sections.translation: Whose murderers ? Where lies the guilt at which you point ?

sections.label: Section 1192
sections.original: τοῖς πατρός · εἶτα τοῖσδε δουλεύω βίᾳ .
sections.translation: The murderers of my father . And , further , I am forced to slave for them .

sections.label: Section 1193
sections.original: τίς γάρ σʼ ἀνάγκῃ τῇδε προτρέπει βροτῶν ;
sections.translation: Who is it that binds you with this compulsion ?

sections.label: Section 1194
sections.original: μήτηρ καλεῖται , μητρὶ δʼ οὐδὲν ἐξισοῖ .
sections.translation: She is called my mother , but in no respect is she like a mother .

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: τί δρῶσα ; πότερα χερσὶν ἢ λύμῃ βίου ;
sections.translation: How does she do it ? By violence or by inflicting hardship ?

sections.label: Section 1196
sections.original: καὶ χερσὶ καὶ λύμαισι καὶ πᾶσιν κακοῖς .
sections.translation: By violence and hardships and all manner of evil .

sections.label: Section 1197
sections.original: οὐδʼ οὑπαρήξων οὐδʼ ὁ κωλύσων πάρα ;
sections.translation: And is there no one to help , or to prevent it ?

sections.label: Section 1198
sections.original: οὐ δῆθʼ . ὃς ἦν γάρ μοι σὺ προὔθηκας σποδόν .
sections.translation: No one . The one I had , his ashes you have put before me .

sections.label: Section 1199
sections.original: ὦ δύσποτμʼ , ὡς ὁρῶν σʼ ἐποικτίρω πάλαι .
sections.translation: Unfortunate girl , how seeing you stirs my pity !

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: μόνος βροτῶν νυν ἴσθʼ ἐποικτίρας ποτέ .
sections.translation: Then know that you are the first who ever pitied me .

sections.label: Section 1201
sections.original: μόνος γὰρ ἥκω τοῖς ἴσοις ἀλγῶν κακοῖς .
sections.translation: Yes , for I alone have come and been pained by your troubles .

sections.label: Section 1202
sections.original: οὐ δή ποθʼ ἡμῖν ξυγγενὴς ἥκεις ποθέν ;
sections.translation: Surely you are not some unknown kinsman ?

sections.label: Section 1203
sections.original: ἐγὼ φράσαιμʼ ἄν , εἰ τὸ τῶνδʼ εὔνουν πάρα .
sections.translation: I would tell you , if these women bear you goodwill .

sections.label: Section 1204
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐστὶν εὔνουν , ὥστε πρὸς πιστὰς ἐρεῖς .
sections.translation: Indeed they do , so you will speak to trustworthy companions .

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: μέθες τόδʼ ἄγγος νῦν , ὅπως τὸ πᾶν μάθῃς .
sections.translation: Give up this urn , then , and you shall know everything .

sections.label: Section 1206
sections.original: μὴ δῆτα πρὸς θεῶν τοῦτό μʼ ἐργάσῃ , ξένε .
sections.translation: No , by the gods , do not do this to me , stranger !

sections.label: Section 1207
sections.original: πείθου λέγοντι κοὐχ ἁμαρτήσει ποτέ .
sections.translation: Do as I say , and you will never be mistaken .

sections.label: Section 1208
sections.original: μή , πρὸς γενείου , μὴ ʼξέλῃ τὰ φίλτατα .
sections.translation: No , I beg you , do not rip from me what I hold most dear !

sections.label: Section 1209
sections.original: οὔ φημʼ ἐάσειν .
sections.translation: You must not keep it .

sections.label: Section 1209a
sections.original: ὦ τάλαινʼ ἐγὼ σέθεν ,
sections.translation: Ah , what misery I will have because of you ,

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: Ὀρέστα , τῆς σῆς εἰ στερήσομαι ταφῆς .
sections.translation: Orestes , if I am robbed of your burial !

sections.label: Section 1211
sections.original: εὔφημα φώνει · πρὸς δίκης γὰρ οὐ στένεις .
sections.translation: Hush ! No ill-omened words ! You have no right to grieve .

sections.label: Section 1212
sections.original: πῶς τὸν θανόντʼ ἀδελφὸν οὐ δίκῃ στένω ;
sections.translation: How is it not right for me to grieve for my dead brother ?

sections.label: Section 1213
sections.original: οὔ σοι προσήκει τήνδε προσφωνεῖν φάτιν .
sections.translation: It is not proper for you to speak of him as you do .

sections.label: Section 1214
sections.original: οὕτως ἄτιμός εἰμι τοῦ τεθνηκότος ;
sections.translation: Am I so without rights in the dead ?

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: ἄτιμος οὐδενὸς σύ · τοῦτο δʼ οὐχὶ σόν .
sections.translation: You are without rights in nothing ; but this burial is not your concern .

sections.label: Section 1216
sections.original: εἴπερ γʼ Ὀρέστου σῶμα βαστάζω τόδε ;
sections.translation: Yes it is , if these are the remains of Orestes that I hold .

sections.label: Section 1217
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκ Ὀρέστου , πλὴν λόγῳ γʼ ἠσκημένον .
sections.translation: They are not his , except inasmuch as fiction alone contrives to make them so . He gently takes the urn from her .

sections.label: Section 1218
sections.original: ποῦ δʼ ἔστʼ ἐκείνου τοῦ ταλαιπώρου τάφος ;
sections.translation: And where is that sufferer ’ s tomb ?

sections.label: Section 1219
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι · τοῦ γὰρ ζῶντος οὐκ ἔστιν τάφος .
sections.translation: There is none ; the living have no tomb .

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας , ὦ παῖ ;
sections.translation: What are you saying , boy ?

sections.label: Section 1221
sections.original: ἦ ζῇ γὰρ ἁνήρ ;
sections.translation: Nothing that is untrue .

sections.label: Section 1221a
sections.original: εἴπερ ἔμψυχός γʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: The man is alive ?

sections.label: Section 1222
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ σὺ κεῖνος ;
sections.translation: If there is life in me .

sections.label: Section 1222a
sections.original: τήνδε προσβλέψασά μου
sections.translation: What ? Are you he ?

sections.label: Section 1223
sections.original: σφραγῖδα πατρὸς ἔκμαθʼ εἰ σαφῆ λέγω .
sections.translation: Look at this signet , once our father ’ s , and know if I speak the truth .

sections.label: Section 1224
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατον φῶς .
sections.translation: O blissful day !

sections.label: Section 1224a
sections.original: φίλτατον , συμμαρτυρῶ .
sections.translation: Blissful , I am your witness !

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: ὦ φθέγμʼ , ἀφίκου ;
sections.translation: Is this your voice ?

sections.label: Section 1225a
sections.original: μηκέτʼ ἄλλοθεν πύθῃ ,
sections.translation: Hear it from no other .

sections.label: Section 1226
sections.original: ἔχω σε χερσίν ;
sections.translation: Do I hold you in my arms ?

sections.label: Section 1226a
sections.original: ὡς τὰ λοίπʼ ἔχοις ἀεί .
sections.translation: May you hold me so always hereafter !

sections.label: Section 1227
sections.original: ὦ φίλταται γυναῖκες , ὦ πολίτιδες ,
sections.translation: Ah , dear friends and fellow-citizens , see Orestes here , who was dead by design , and now by design has come safely home !

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: ὁρῶμεν , ὦ παῖ , κἀπὶ συμφοραῖσί μοι
sections.translation: We see him , daughter , and for this happy turn of fortune a tear of joy trickles from our eyes .

sections.label: Section 1.1232
sections.original: ἰὼ γοναί ,
sections.translation: O seed , seed of the person to me most dear , you have just now come ,

sections.label: Section 1.1235
sections.original: ἐφηύρετʼ , ἤλθετʼ , εἴδεθʼ οὓς ἐχρῄζετε .
sections.translation: you have come , and have found and seen her whom your heart desired !

sections.label: Section 1.1236
sections.original: πάρεσμεν · ἀλλὰ σῖγʼ ἔχουσα πρόσμενε .
sections.translation: I am with you ; but keep silence and wait .
